citations.description: search_files for 'link_direction' across tests/: no matches
search_files for 'REB-458' across tests/: no matches
search_files for 'REB-461' across tests/: no matches

findings.criterion: A `_diff_links_inbound` unit test on the CAPTURED LIVE REB-458 issuelinks JSON (outwardIssue Blocks) asserts relation == `blocks`; on REB-461 JSON (inwardIssue Blocks) asserts relation == `depends_on`. RED on current code. Proving command: `pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/ -k link_direction -q`.
findings.detail: No test matching `-k link_direction` exists anywhere in the test suite. Searched `tests/` for 'link_direction', 'REB-458', 'REB-461', and found zero matches. The requirement demands absolute-direction tests pinned to captured live-Jira fixtures; none are present.

citations.description: search_files for 'inverse' in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/: no matches
search_files for 'counterpart' in tests/: no matches

findings.criterion: A `_diff_links_inbound` test with the counterpart owning the inverse edge asserts 0 emitted mutations (inverse-aware dedup). RED on a pure-swap fix.
findings.detail: No such test exists in the diffing test tree. Additionally, the `_diff_links_inbound` implementation does NOT perform cross-ticket inverse-aware dedup — it only checks `existing_deps` from the local ticket itself and does not consult the counterpart ticket's deps from `local_tickets_by_id`. The `compute_inbound_mutations` function's cross-ticket suppression is limited to outbound-mutation coordination (bug 3bf8), not counterpart inverse-edge detection.

citations.description: search_files for 'absolute_direction' in tests/: only 1 match in external live tests (test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py), none in unit tests for _diff_link_removals

findings.criterion: The removal-path mirror (`_diff_link_removals`) has an equivalent absolute-direction test. RED on current code.
findings.detail: test_link_removal_sync.py tests the removal-path direction logic (test_managed_outward_blocks_removed_maps_to_depends_on) but does NOT pin to a captured live-Jira fixture (REB-458/461 JSON or equivalent ground-truth capture). The AC requires an absolute-direction test for the removal path analogous to the REB-458/461 test required for the add path; no such test exists.

citations.description: list_directory of src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler: no link_direction.py present
Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'outbound_links.py + acli_graph.py kept their own copies. FOLLOW-UP change 778 completes it. CLOSE 4b59 once 778 lands + verifier passes.'

findings.criterion: UNIFY the duplicated direction logic into ONE helper (`_JIRA_LINK_TO_RELATION` is re-declared in acli_graph.py:53, inbound_differ.py:459, and mirrors in outbound_links.py). One source of truth.
findings.detail: `_JIRA_LINK_TO_RELATION` is still declared in three separate files: acli_graph.py:53, inbound_differ.py:459, and outbound_links.py:45. No `link_direction.py` (or equivalent single-source helper) exists. The ticket comment explicitly acknowledges this: 'the inbound path was unified into link_direction.py but outbound_links.py + acli_graph.py kept their own copies. FOLLOW-UP change 778 (fix/unify-link-direction) completes it.' That follow-up has not landed.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
